ogTitle: West Pennant Hills vs West Ryde: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in West Pennant Hills and West Ryde.
heading: West Pennant Hills vs West Ryde — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between West Pennant Hills, New South Wales and West Ryde, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: West Pennant Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to West Ryde (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is West Pennant Hills or West Ryde safer from flooding?
faq.answer: West Pennant Hills has a flood risk score of 38/100, while West Ryde scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: West Pennant Hills scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. West Ryde scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in West Pennant Hills or West Ryde?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. West Pennant Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.12% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
